description: Open hand, fingers together, palm facing outward. Bring hand up to forehead then sweep outward away from forehead in a salute-like motion. Used as a standard greeting in ASL.
source: ASL University (Lifeprint.com) - Dr. Bill Vicars

description: Flat hand, fingers together, palm facing up. Touch fingertips to chin then move hand forward and slightly down away from face. Similar to blowing a kiss outward.
source: ASL University (Lifeprint.com) - Dr. Bill Vicars

description: Hand in S-handshape (closed fist). Nod the fist up and down at the wrist, mimicking a head nod. Performed at approximately shoulder height.
movement: Repeated up-down nodding motion at wrist
source: ASL University (Lifeprint.com) - Dr. Bill Vicars

description: Extend index and middle fingers (or index finger and thumb). Snap them together twice in a quick pinching motion, like a mouth saying no.
source: ASL University (Lifeprint.com) - Dr. Bill Vicars

description: Hand in A-handshape (closed fist with thumb alongside). Make circular rubbing motion on the chest area in a clockwise direction. Represents rubbing your heart.
source: ASL University (Lifeprint.com) - Dr. Bill Vicars

description: Extend thumb, index finger, and pinky simultaneously while keeping ring and middle fingers folded down. Hold the hand up facing outward. This is a combination of I, L, and Y handshapes merged into one iconic sign.
handshape: ILY handshape - thumb + index + pinky extended
location: Neutral space in front of body
source: ASL University (Lifeprint.com) - Dr. Bill Vicars

description: Closed fist with thumb resting alongside the index finger on the side. The thumb is visible and not tucked under. This is the ASL fingerspelling letter A.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Four fingers straight up and together, thumb tucked across palm. The hand is flat and vertical like a stop sign but with the thumb folded in.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Fingers and thumb curved to form a C-shape, like holding a cylinder. All fingers together, curved with thumb below forming an open circle shape.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index finger points straight up, middle, ring, and pinky fingers curve to touch the thumb, forming a circular shape with a vertical index finger.
handshape: Index up, other fingers and thumb form circle
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: All four fingers curl down at the second joint, like a bent claw. Thumb tucks under the bent fingers. Resembles a closed claw or bent-finger shape.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index finger and thumb touch at the tips forming a circle. Middle, ring, and pinky fingers extend straight up. Circle formed by index and thumb with three fingers up.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index finger and thumb extend pointing horizontally to the side, parallel to the ground. Other fingers folded. Like pointing sideways with index finger and thumb extended.
handshape: Index and thumb horizontal, others folded
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index and middle fingers extend together horizontally pointing to the side. Ring and pinky fingers and thumb folded in. Two fingers pointing sideways parallel to ground.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Pinky finger extended straight up, all other fingers and thumb closed in a fist. Only the pinky is up.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Start with pinky extended (I-hand), then trace a J-shape in the air — move down then curve upward and to the right. This is a MOTION sign unlike all other alphabet letters.
movement: Traces J-shape in air, down then curved up
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index finger points up, middle finger angled outward and forward, thumb between index and middle fingers pointing forward. Like a peace sign but with thumb between the fingers.
handshape: Index up, middle angled, thumb between
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index finger points straight up, thumb extends horizontally to the side forming an L-shape. Other fingers folded down. Classic L-shape with index and thumb.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Three fingers (index, middle, ring) folded down over the tucked thumb. Pinky also folded. The thumb is tucked under three bent fingers.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Two fingers (index, middle) folded down over the tucked thumb. Ring and pinky folded. The thumb is tucked under two bent fingers.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: All fingers and thumb curve to meet each other at the tips, forming a circle or O-shape. Like holding a small ball with all fingertips touching the thumb.
handshape: All fingertips and thumb meet forming O
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Like K-handshape but rotated so the hand points downward. Index finger pointing down, middle finger angled, thumb between index and middle. A downward-pointing K.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Like G-handshape but rotated downward. Index finger and thumb pointing down. Other fingers folded. A downward-pointing G.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index and middle fingers extended and crossed over each other. Ring and pinky folded down, thumb tucked. The two extended fingers are intertwined/crossed.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: All fingers curled into a fist with the thumb wrapped in front across the fingers. Thumb covers the front of the curled fingers. Tight fist with thumb in front.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index finger folds down with thumb tucked between index and middle fingers from underneath. Thumb tip visible between the two fingers.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index and middle fingers extended straight up together, held close and parallel. Ring, pinky, and thumb folded. Two fingers up together pointing upward.
handshape: Index and middle together, pointing up
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index and middle fingers extended and spread apart forming a V-shape or peace sign. Ring, pinky, and thumb folded. Classic V for victory or peace sign.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index, middle, and ring fingers extended and spread apart forming a W-shape. Pinky and thumb folded. Three fingers spread like a W or a three-pronged shape.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index finger extended and bent or hooked at the first joint like a hook. Other fingers and thumb folded. A bent/hooked index finger.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Thumb and pinky extended outward, middle fingers folded down. Like a Y-shape or a shaka/hang-loose gesture. Thumb out, pinky out, three middle fingers folded.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart

description: Index finger extended, traces a Z-shape in the air. Move right, then diagonally down-left, then right again. This is a motion sign like J.
source: Gallaudet University ASL Connect - Standard Fingerspelling Chart
